title: anthyllis vulneraria f. versicolor
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria f. versicolor, also known as kidney vetch or sensitive grass, is a perennial legume plant that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and glycosides. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Anthyllis vulneraria f. versicolor can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support skin health and treat minor wounds.
